issues.title: getPillClass helper recreated on every render in ProductFilters
issues.description: getPillClass is defined as a plain function inside the component body, so it is recreated on every render. It is called multiple times in the render output (5 call-sites). For a debounced filter component that re-renders on input, this is a minor overhead.
issues.fix: Either define getPillClass outside the component (it has no closure dependencies) or wrap with useCallback. The simplest fix is to hoist it: `const getPillClass = (isActive: boolean) => cn(...)` before the component declaration, since it does not use any props or state.

issues.title: Client-side filtering of all products on every render in ProductCatalogPage
issues.description: filteredProducts is already correctly wrapped in useMemo. However, the search filter iterates over the full product list and calls i18nField() (which accesses both name_en and name_es fields) for every product on every keystroke that invalidates the memo. This is fine at small catalog sizes, but will degrade with larger catalogs. The filtering is purely client-side — all products are loaded upfront via useStoreProducts().
issues.fix: This is a known trade-off of the current architecture (full client-side filtering). No immediate action required. If the catalog grows significantly, consider moving filtering server-side or adding pagination/virtualization. The useMemo is correctly in place to prevent re-computation on unrelated renders.

issues.title: Inline style objects for dynamic colors create new object references on every render
issues.description: Multiple components use inline `style={{ backgroundColor: categoryColor }}` and similar patterns. Because each render creates a new object literal, React must re-evaluate style equality on every render of the parent list. This affects ProductCardImage, ProductBadges, CartItemRow, HeroSection, MobileBar, and several inline-editor components. In list contexts (product grid), each card render creates fresh object references. The values come from static lookup maps (getCategoryColor/getCategoryTheme), so the computed value is stable for a given category.
issues.fix: For list items (ProductCardImage, ProductBadges, CartItemRow), consider either: (1) accepting this as a pragmatic trade-off given React's fast object comparison, or (2) wrapping the computed style object with useMemo when the color derivation is stable per category. The highest-impact case is ProductCardImage since it renders in a grid of many items. Example: `const imgStyle = useMemo(() => ({ backgroundColor: categoryColor }), [categoryColor])`.

issues.title: FeaturedRibbon canvas animation runs requestAnimationFrame loop continuously
issues.description: The FeaturedRibbon component uses a requestAnimationFrame loop to animate a shine effect on a canvas. The loop runs at 60fps for all visible featured product cards simultaneously. When multiple featured products are on-screen (e.g. in the catalog page), each ribbon runs its own continuous RAF loop. The cleanup on unmount/deps change is correctly handled (`cancelAnimationFrame` + `themeObserver.disconnect()`). This is a design choice for the visual effect, not a bug, but worth noting for performance budgets.
issues.fix: Consider using an IntersectionObserver to pause the RAF loop when the ribbon is not in the viewport, or switch to a CSS animation for the shine effect (which the browser can optimize and batch). For the current catalog scale this is acceptable. If performance profiling shows frame-rate drops on the catalog page, this is the first place to investigate.

patterns: All setInterval and setTimeout usages in the codebase correctly return cleanup functions (clearInterval/clearTimeout) inside useEffect — no memory leaks found.
No raw tags found outside test files — all image usage in production components correctly uses Next.js next/image.

No React.lazy() or next/dynamic() usage found in apps/ — no lazy-loaded components. Given the current app structure this appears intentional; heavy third-party libraries (recharts, framer-motion, dnd-kit) were not found in apps/.
new Date() inside render only occurs in a click handler (handleExportCsv in UsersPageContent) for generating a filename timestamp — not a performance concern.
The two setInterval-based timers (ExpirationLabel, ReceivedOrderCard) are correctly implemented with useMemo for the date object and clearInterval cleanup.
Debounce hooks (SearchBar, ProductFilters, UsersPageContent, useAutoSavePaymentMethod) all properly clean up timers via useEffect return functions.
